shortAnswer: ESR (erythrocyte sedimentation rate) measures how quickly red blood cells settle in a blood sample. An elevated value may indicate inflammation, infection, or other conditions. ESR is a non-specific marker and is usually assessed alongside other tests.
metaDescription: ESR elevated? Normal ranges, causes, and meaning of erythrocyte sedimentation rate — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What condition could explain my elevated ESR?
Should I also check CRP and other values?
pageTitle: ESR value — Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate explained | Medyra
